claim: Benchmark leaderboards show models improving on narrow tasks, but real-world dissatisfaction rate (users disliking both options) remains ~9% even for top models
actionable: Don't over-rely on benchmark scores; test frontier models on your actual use cases as performance varies significantly by task type
source_title: What Do Models Still Suck At? - Peter Gostev, Arena.ai, BullshitBench

claim: Models hallucinate by going along with nonsensical premises rather than pushing back; Claude/Sonnet models do this better than GPT/Gemini (50/50 pushback rate)
actionable: When deploying models, expect them to sometimes accept false premises; use Claude for critical tasks requiring rejection of invalid inputs
source_title: What Do Models Still Suck At? - Peter Gostev, Arena.ai, BullshitBench

claim: Increased reasoning (thinking) on nonsense questions often makes model responses worse, not better - they spend paragraphs justifying false premises
actionable: Don't blindly enable reasoning mode; test if it helps or hurts on your specific tasks
source_title: What Do Models Still Suck At? - Peter Gostev, Arena.ai, BullshitBench

claim: Model scale (parameters) doesn't correlate with performance on nonsense/reasoning tasks; bigger isn't always better
actionable: When selecting models, don't assume larger=better; test on your actual requirements
source_title: What Do Models Still Suck At? - Peter Gostev, Arena.ai, BullshitBench

claim: Expert-rated prompts show improvement in quantitative tasks (math, physics) but limited progress in creative/gaming/design domains
actionable: For analytical work, models have improved significantly; for creative/design work, progress is slower - manage expectations accordingly
source_title: What Do Models Still Suck At? - Peter Gostev, Arena.ai, BullshitBench

claim: Gaming/game design is a domain where LLMs show poor understanding of mechanics, challenge balance, and entertainment - models generate mechanics that aren't interesting
actionable: Don't rely on LLMs for game design; they lack understanding of game feel and engaging mechanics
source_title: What Do Models Still Suck At? - Peter Gostev, Arena.ai, BullshitBench

claim: Large benchmark improvements don't translate to equivalent improvements in messy real-world tasks; bottom of distribution matters more than top-line metrics
actionable: Focus evaluation on consistent performance across task types, not just best-case scenarios captured in benchmarks
source_title: What Do Models Still Suck At? - Peter Gostev, Arena.ai, BullshitBench

claim: User expectations shift over time - what counted as acceptable 3 years ago is unacceptable now, making static benchmarks misleading
actionable: Update your evaluation criteria regularly as user expectations and task complexity increase
source_title: What Do Models Still Suck At? - Peter Gostev, Arena.ai, BullshitBench
